slides.title: OBJECTIVE
slides.textContent:
OBJECTIVE
● Automate the end-to-end machine learning workflow including data preprocessing, model training,
evaluation, and selection.
● Develop a Python package that simplifies ML tasks for beginners and professionals by abstracting
repetitive code.
● Support both classification and regression tasks with automatic handling of missing values, encoding,
scaling, and feature selection.
● Integrate multiple machine learning algorithms and provide a leaderboard for performance comparison.
● Enable easy deployment and usability by publishing the package on PyPI for one-line installation.

slides.title: INTRODUCTION
slides.textContent:
INTRODUCTION
🔍 Why MLBuddy?
● ML pipelines are time-consuming and error-prone.
● Manual steps like preprocessing, model selection & evaluation can be tedious and
discourage users.
● MLBuddy automates repetitive tasks so users focus on insights, not setup.
⚙ Core Features
● Automates full ML lifecycle: cleaning, feature transformation, training, evaluation.
● Reduces human error and speeds up experimentation.
● Supports both classification and regression tasks.

slides.title: INTRODUCTION
slides.textContent:
INTRODUCTION
📈 Smart Model Evaluation
● Automatically trains & evaluates multiple models.
● Built-in leaderboard shows model performance (accuracy, recall, R², etc.).
● Helps identify promising models quickly for further tuning.
🧰 Designed for Everyone
● Simple Python API for coders.
● Streamlit no-code app for non-programmers.
● Handles missing values, encoding, and scaling automatically.

slides.title: INTRODUCTION
slides.textContent:
INTRODUCTION
🚀 User-Friendly and Scalable
● Published on PyPI for easy installation.
● Requires minimal code to run end-to-end workflows.
● Ideal for fast prototyping and real-world deployment.
🎯 Project Goal
● Make ML workflows consistent, replicable, and less tedious.
● Enable users to explore and experiment more—without getting stuck in setup.
● Support impactful, data-driven decision making with minimal overhead.

slides.title: Problem Statement
slides.textContent:
Problem Statement
🚧 Challenges in Traditional ML Workflows:
● Time-consuming and repetitive coding for cleaning, feature engineering, model
selection & evaluation.
● Fragmented tools require mixing libraries & scripts, causing inefficiencies and
integration issues.
● Steep learning curve discourages beginners due to coding and ML algorithm
knowledge requirements.
● Model comparison is difficult without unified evaluation across metrics and models.
● Slow prototyping and testing , delaying insights in fast-paced environments.
